mapping.a_element: Monte Carlo subsampling used to check robustness of the denoised portfolios
mapping.b_element: Monte Carlo experiments used to estimate the probability P(k) of pocketing k balls

mapping.a_element: separating a structured/signal component from a random/noise component
mapping.b_element: distinguishing a positive-measure (open) set of clearing strokes from a measure-zero fluke

brief_justification: The core machinery differs: paper A uses random-matrix-theory spectral decomposition with Marchenko-Pastur eigenvalue bounds to denoise a correlation matrix and recover network structure, whereas paper B uses billiard dynamics plus a Lebesgue-measure existence argument (measure-zero vs unobservably-small) in shot space. The only overlap is generic use of Monte Carlo sampling and a loose signal-vs-noise theme, with no role-for-role correspondence between eigenvalue thresholds and measure-theoretic shot sets, so they do not share transferable machinery.
